artPrompt: An endless queue of slumped townsfolk silhouettes snaking through a velvet-rope maze toward a distant service window that never grows closer, the line vanishing into cyan-lit fluorescent haze. A wall ticket dispenser shows a blank smudged numeral; weary shadows pool long across the ivory floor. Steep receding perspective makes the front of the line impossibly far, oppressive and still, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: The Line That Never Moves
text:
Each opponent's spells and abilities go to the back of the line: when an opponent would cast a spell or activate an ability, they instead place it in the queue. At the beginning of that player's next upkeep, the oldest thing in their queue finally resolves — one per turn, no more. The line does not move faster because you sighed.
The queue has no maximum length. It has never had an end.
flavorText: You were eleventh in line at 9 a.m. It is now next week. You are eleventh in line.
